summary: Updated the repository README so the Code Progress and Data Progress shields are clickable links to the Melee progress page on progress.decomp.club. This was a documentation/README usability change only, with no source or matching impact.
what_changed: Changed the Code Progress badge from a plain image to a linked Markdown image pointing to https://progress.decomp.club/data/melee/.
Changed the Data Progress badge from a plain image to a linked Markdown image pointing to https://progress.decomp.club/data/melee/.
Kept the existing shields.io endpoint URLs for the badge images, including measure=code and measure=data.
smart_moves: Preserved the existing badge visual endpoints while adding navigation, avoiding any disruption to the displayed code/data progress values.
Linked both progress badges to the central Melee progress page rather than exposing only the shield endpoint, making the README more useful for contributors and observers.
decomp_lessons: README progress indicators should be both visible and actionable; link progress shields to the underlying progress dashboard when available.
Small repository presentation improvements can improve contributor onboarding without affecting matching work or build outputs.
naming_conventions: Badge labels remained `Code Progress` and `Data Progress` in the Markdown alt text.
The shields.io endpoint labels remained `Code` and `Data` via the `label=` query parameter.
The project progress route used the existing decomp.club Melee path: `progress.decomp.club/data/melee/`.
follow_up_queries: Find README changes that update progress.decomp.club links or shields.io progress badge endpoints.
Find documentation-only PRs that improve contributor visibility into decompilation progress.
Check whether badge links in project READMEs point to useful dashboards rather than only image endpoints.
